name: Bachmann Bundle Block
identifyBy: P wave duration ≥120ms (broad, similar to P-mitrale but different mechanism)
Biphasic P wave morphology in inferior leads (positive then negative) — advanced IAB (Bayes criterion)
P wave changes throughout leads as interatrial conduction timing is altered
First degree IAB: P wave duration ≥120ms only (similar to P-mitrale)
Advanced IAB: P wave biphasic in inferior leads (positive-negative) — much higher AF risk

measurements.pWaves: Broad ≥120ms; biphasic in inferior leads (advanced IAB)

management: Bachmann bundle block itself does not require direct treatment
Advanced IAB (biphasic P in inferior leads): strong predictor of AF and cardioembolic stroke
Bayes syndrome: advanced IAB + AF history = high stroke risk — cardiology referral for anticoagulation decision
Screen for: LV dysfunction, LA enlargement, ischaemic heart disease (common associations)
Echocardiogram: assess LA size and LV function
Holter monitoring: AF detection if symptomatic (palpitations, unexplained stroke)
pearls: Bachmann's bundle is the principal interatrial conduction pathway — connects right to left atrium
Advanced IAB (biphasic P inferior leads) is associated with AF, stroke, and atrial cardiomyopathy
Bayes syndrome = advanced IAB + history of AF → high stroke risk even if currently in sinus rhythm
Distinguishing P-mitrale from Bachmann block: P-mitrale has bifid/notched P in II but positive throughout; IAB has biphasic (pos-neg) P in inferior leads
P-wave duration ≥120ms is the ECG hallmark of BOTH P-mitrale and IAB — lead V1 pattern and clinical context differentiate
differentials: P-mitrale (broad bifid P, positive throughout inferior leads, mitral valve disease)
Biatrial enlargement (both tall and broad P)
WPW with negative delta in inferior leads (short PR distinguishes)
Normal P wave variant with right atrial overload in pulmonary disease
